marketFocus.focus: Warsh Fed Nomination, Historic Metals Collapse, US Government Shutdown
headlines: Trump nominates Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair—hawkish balance sheet stance, no rate cut commitment
Gold -8.6%, Silver -16%—historic precious metals sell-off continues into Asian session
US partial government shutdown weighs on sentiment, equity futures at session lows
NVIDIA halts $100bn OpenAI investment plan, tech sector under pressure
marketFocus.macroContext: Risk-off sentiment dominates as markets digest Warsh's Fed Chair nomination amid ongoing precious metals collapse. Chinese PMIs slip into contraction (Manufacturing 50.3, Services 51.8), adding demand-side concerns. US government shutdown creates policy uncertainty. Defensive positioning prevails across asset classes.

tradingSummary: USD rangebound despite Warsh hawkish premium—government shutdown caps gains. Commodities crushed: Gold -8.6%, Copper -4.6%, crude benchmarks down 5%+. Safe-havens (JPY, CHF) outperform. EUR/GBP pressured ahead of manufacturing PMI finals. Asia-Pac equities down 1-2%, materials sector hammered. Focus shifts to ISM Manufacturing PMI (16:00) and RBA policy decision (Tue 04:30).

USD.summary: Warsh nomination injects hawkish Fed premium but partial government shutdown limits upside. DXY +0.1% at 97.22, rangebound. Warsh viewed as balance sheet hawk with no rate cut signals—market recalibrates terminal rate expectations. Fed speakers divided: Musalem says no more cuts, policy neutral; Waller dissented for 25bps cut citing job market risks. ISM Manufacturing PMI (16:00) key test—forecast 48.3 vs 47.9 prior. 1-3d: Choppy consolidation, Warsh rhetoric and ISM data drive direction.
headlines: Trump nominates Warsh as Fed Chair
Fed's Musalem: No more cuts, policy neutral
USD.reaction: USD modestly firmer on Warsh hawkish expectations, but shutdown caps rally. Yields steepen.

EUR.summary: EUR/USD at 1.1850, below 1.19 resistance. Manufacturing PMI Finals (09:50-10:00) likely confirm contraction—France 49.1, Germany 49.5 expected. German Retail Sales (Dec) -0.2% M/M disappoints. ECB policy meeting Thursday looms with 25bps cut fully priced. Risk-off sentiment and weak data flow weigh. French inflation data (Wed) and ECB Lagarde presser (Thu 15:30) key catalysts ahead. 1-3d: Downside bias, PMI misses and ECB dovish tilt amplify weakness.
EUR.reaction: EUR pressured ahead of PMI confirmations, ECB cut expectations weigh.

GBP.summary: GBP/USD -0.1% at 1.3665, consolidating. UK Manufacturing PMI Final (10:30) expected 51.6 vs 50.6 prior—modest improvement but still weak. BoE's Breeden (neutral-dovish) keynote at 12:45 may hint at future easing path. BoE policy meeting Friday—no cut expected (4.50% forecast vs 4.75% current) but dovish signals likely. Nationwide Housing Prices +1.0% Y/Y (vs 0.6% prior) offers minor support. 1-3d: Range-bound, BoE preview and PMI data drive near-term moves.
GBP.reaction: GBP steady but cautious ahead of BoE, PMI data and Breeden speech watched.

JPY.summary: USD/JPY +0.1% at 154.81 but off highs—Takaichi remarks initially boosted pair but subsequent clarification unwound move. Risk-off sentiment drives safe-haven flows to JPY. BoJ Summary of Opinions: One member says financial conditions remain accommodative even after rate hike. JGBs benefit from latest election polling. Metals collapse and equity weakness support JPY. BoJ hike expectations (Mar/Apr) underpin currency. 1-3d: Bullish, risk-off tailwind strong, carry unwind accelerates.
JPY.reaction: JPY rallies on safe-haven demand, risk-off dominates despite Takaichi noise.

AUD.summary: AUD crushed by metals sell-off—Spot Gold -8.6%, LME Copper -4.6%, base metals hammered. Chinese PMIs disappoint: Manufacturing 50.3 (in-line but weak), Services 51.8 vs 52.0 prior—demand concerns mount. RBA policy decision Tuesday 04:30—hike odds fade as commodity rout intensifies. ASX 200 -1.0%, materials sector leads losses. Risk-off and China demand fears = AUD weakness. 1-3d: Bearish, metals sell-off dominates, RBA decision unlikely to rescue.
AUD.reaction: AUD crushed by historic metals sell-off, China data adds to woes.

CAD.summary: CAD pressured by crude benchmarks collapse—WTI -5.6%, Brent -5.4% alongside APAC stocks. OPEC+ maintained pause as expected but demand fears outweigh supply discipline. US-Canada trade tensions simmer (Trump tariff rhetoric). Metals crash (Gold/Silver) hits Canadian mining sector. Risk-off sentiment weighs. BoC on hold, Macklem focused on tariff risks. 1-3d: Bearish, crude weakness and risk-off dominate.
CAD.reaction: CAD pressured by crude weakness, risk-off amplifies losses.

CHF.summary: CHF benefits from risk-off flows as metals collapse and equity weakness intensify. EUR/CHF dips as EUR weakness compounds. Swiss Retail Sales +1.0% M/M (vs -0.1% prior) and +2.9% Y/Y (vs 1.7% prior) beat expectations. procure.ch Manufacturing PMI 48.8 vs 45.8 prior—improvement but still contraction. SNB dovish tilt offset by safe-haven demand. 1-3d: Bullish, safe-haven bid strong, EUR weakness supports.
CHF.reaction: CHF rallies on safe-haven demand, risk-off dominates.

NZD.summary: NZD hammered by risk-off and commodity weakness. Metals crash (Gold/Silver) and China PMI disappointment hit sentiment. NZ employment data ahead (Tue 22:45)—Unemployment Rate forecast 5.3% (unchanged), Employment Change +0.3% Q/Q. RBNZ cut expectations (Feb/Mar) weigh. Carry unwind accelerates. Building Permits data (Mon 22:45) unlikely to move needle. 1-3d: Bearish, risk-off and commodity rout dominate, employment data key.
NZD.reaction: NZD hammered by risk-off, commodity weakness amplifies losses.
